welcome.title: Welcome to LifeGift
welcome.subtitle: A calm place to check in about symptoms during your cancer treatment.
welcome.description: LifeGift can help you understand whether a symptom is safe to watch, worth calling your care team about, or needs urgent attention — based on the same guidance your 24-hour oncology helpline uses.
welcome.emergencyNotice: This does not replace emergency care. If you think this is a medical emergency, call 999 now.
welcome.noAccountNeeded: No account or sign-in needed to get started.

cancerTreatment.title: A bit about your treatment
cancerTreatment.subtitle: This helps us give advice that fits your situation. You can change this later.
cancerTreatment.recentTreatmentQuestion: Have you had any cancer treatment (including tablets) in the last 6 weeks?

helpline.title: One last thing
helpline.subtitle: So the right number is always within reach when you need it.

cancerTypePicker.question: What type of cancer are you being treated for?

treatmentTypePicker.question: What treatment are you currently receiving?

helplineInput.question: What's your 24-hour oncology helpline number?
helplineInput.hint: This is on your treatment alert card. We'll keep it one tap away at the top of every screen.
helplineInput.invalid: That doesn't look like a phone number yet.

chat.greeting: Hi! I can see you're being treated for {cancerType} cancer. Tell me what's going on and I'll help you figure out the next step.
chat.failSafeMessage: Sorry, something went wrong on our end. To be safe, please contact your 24-hour helpline to discuss your symptoms.
chat.safetyNetMessage: Before we finish — is there anything else going on, like a temperature, nausea, or unusual bleeding? If so, just tell me.
chat.startNewConversationConfirm: Start a new conversation? This clears your chat history on this device.

redFlag.title: Go to A&E now
redFlag.body: Based on what you've told us, this needs urgent medical attention right away — please don't wait to see if it improves.
